channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia governor declares storm emergency as snow and ice bear down on mid-Atlantic states
item.description: A wintry mess was bearing down on mid-Atlantic states Tuesday with forecasts of significant snow and ice accumulations prompting warnings of potential power outages.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Migrants stranded in Mexico try to restart life after Trump eliminates legal pathway to US
item.description: Many migrants have been left stranded in Mexican border cities after the Trump administration immediately canceled tens of thosands of appointments made through a government app called CBP One that offered a legal pathway to the U.S. Some have returned to their countries.
item.dc:creator: Julie Watson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's education chief comes from the boardroom, not the classroom. Backers say that's her strength
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon once described herself as an outsider stepping into the world of education, a business leader with an “inquiring mind” who would challenge the status quo and demand more for students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: America's kids are still behind in reading and math. These schools are defying the trend
item.description: COMPTON, Calif. (AP) — Math is the subject sixth grader Harmoni Knight finds hardest, but that's changing. In-class tutors and “data chats” at her middle school in Compton, California, have made a dramatic difference, the 11-year-old said.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about proposals to ban abortion pills and punish women who seek abortion
item.description: Lawmakers in some states are pushing measures to crack down on abortion pills or penalize women who obtain abortions.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hegseth renames North Carolina military base Fort Roland L. Bragg
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth signed an order Monday restoring the name of a storied special operations forces base back to Fort Bragg.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump names Grenell as interim Kennedy Center executive director
item.description: President Trump on Monday named Richard Grenell, his envoy for special missions, the interim executive director of the Kennedy Center days after he named himself chair of a new board. “I am pleased to announce that Ric Grenell will serve as the Interim Executive Director of The Kennedy Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Recent aviation disasters cause fears about the safety of flying
item.description: The spate of recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington, the fiery plane crash in Philadelphia and a plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 are only the most high-profile disasters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A$AP Rocky may take the risk of testifying at his felony assault trial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A$AP Rocky and his attorneys revealed that they may have the hip-hop star take the stand Tuesday in his own defense at his trial over two felony counts of assault with a semiautomatic firearm.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hawaii court rules against insurance companies in Maui wildfire, allowing $4B settlement to proceed
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii’s Supreme Court ruled Monday that insurance companies can’t bring their own legal actions against those blamed for Maui’s catastrophic 2023 wildfire, allowing a $4 billion settlement that was on that the verge of collapse to proceed.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Labor unions sue to block DOGE access to sensitive information at US agencies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of labor unions filed a lawsuit Monday asking a federal court to stop Elon Musk’s team from accessing private data at the Education Department, the Treasury Department and the Office of Personnel Management.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against New York Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: The Justice Department has ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York Mayor Eric Adams and halt the ongoing investigation.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs off on new steel and aluminum tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed proclamations to bolster tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the United States and crack down on attempts by China and Russia to evade penalties. Trump in 2018 announced 25 percent tariffs on steel and 10 percent tariffs on aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested in Virginia for allegedly trespassing at three schools
item.description: Zachary Cruz, the brother of the Parkland shooter Nikolas Cruz was arrested last week for allegedly trespassing at three Virginia schools.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecutor says Salman Rushdie was too stunned to react when a man started to stab him
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Salman Rushdie was so stunned when a masked man started to stab him on a stage in western New York that the author didn't even try to fight back, a prosecutor said Monday during opening statements in the suspect's attempted murder trial.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the possibility of Trump serving a third term
item.description: Murmurs from President Trump and his allies have stirred up questions of the incumbent possibly seeking a third term in office, even as he’s currently constitutionally barred from doing so.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Catalytic converters stolen from Illinois district's school buses, forcing students to stay home
item.description: A suburban Illinois school district was forced to hold an emergency e-learning day after the catalytic converters were stolen from "over 80%" of its buses.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge extends restraining order to ban Trump admin buyout offer to federal workers
item.description: A federal judge on Monday extended a temporary restraining related to the Trump administration's plan to offer buyout to thousands of federal employees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested for trespassing at three schools
item.description: Surveillance footage recovered from Jan. 25 showed him attempting to open exterior doors and peering into the schools.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Sutherland, NewsNation

item.title: Family of Chesapeake man missing since last July still hoping for answers
item.description: "He's been gone six months now, and it's just hard to go on without him," Cynthia Wolf, Bill Wolf's wife said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: Judge directs Trump administration to comply with order to unfreeze federal grants
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island ordered the Trump administration to comply with his order to unfreeze federal grants, after attorneys general for several Democratic states claimed the directive was not being fulfilled. In a short order Monday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance proves pivotal in pushing through controversial Trump nominees
item.description: Vice President Vance has quickly become President Trump's liaison to the Senate, which has paid dividends as the GOP-led body moves to confirm even Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: A third federal judge indefinitely blocked President Trump’s executive order to restrict birthright citizenship, dealing another stark blow to the controversial directive. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Jon Batiste's reimagined Super Bowl anthem highlighted wife's painting and set stage for new music
item.description: Jon Batiste transformed the Super Bowl stage into a symphony of creativity, debuting new music, reimagining “The Star-Spangled Banner" and shining a global spotlight on his wife's creative vision.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Eagles deny the Chiefs a Super Bowl three-peat with dominant defense in a 40-22 rout
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Yo Philly: Jalen Hurts and Nick Sirianni are world champions. A ferocious Philadelphia Eagles defense tormented and frustrated Patrick Mahomes while Hurts made all the plays the offense needed. So much for the Kansas City Chiefs’ quest for a Super Bowl three-peat.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are focusing on the wreckage of a plane that crashed off western Alaska as they try to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea, killing 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nelly delivers hits at 'Homecoming' Super Bowl week concert in historic New Orleans restaurant
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Nelly brought his signature St. Louis swagger to a New Orleans institution, turning a nearly seven-decade-old restaurant into a Super Bowl week party. The multi-Grammy winner packed Brennan’s on Friday night, delivering a hit-filled set at the h.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ appeals block of birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: It's the first Justice Department appeal of a lawsuit challenging major Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4
